aws-managed-flink:position-deletes:v2.notes: Flink supports reading and writing position deletes in both batch and streaming modes
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application

aws-managed-flink:position-deletes:v3.notes: Position deletes supported via Iceberg library V3 support (iceberg-flink-runtime 1.10.x+)
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application

aws-managed-flink:equality-deletes:v2.notes: Flink supports equality deletes via UPSERT mode with primary keys; used for streaming CDC and upsert workloads
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application

aws-managed-flink:equality-deletes:v3.notes: Equality deletes supported via Iceberg library V3 support (iceberg-flink-runtime 1.10.x+)
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application

aws-managed-flink:merge-on-read:v2.notes: Flink supports merge-on-read via streaming upsert mode using equality deletes
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application

aws-managed-flink:merge-on-read:v3.notes: Merge-on-read supported via Iceberg library V3 support including deletion vectors
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application

aws-managed-flink:copy-on-write:v2.notes: Copy-on-write supported via INSERT INTO and INSERT OVERWRITE
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application

aws-managed-flink:copy-on-write:v3.notes: Copy-on-write supported via INSERT INTO and INSERT OVERWRITE with V3 format
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application

aws-managed-flink:schema-evolution:v2.notes: Flink can read tables with evolved schemas, but ALTER TABLE only supports changing table properties — column and partition changes are not supported via Flink DDL
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application
ALTER TABLE only supports changing table properties; column additions, renames, drops, and reordering not supported via Flink SQL

aws-managed-flink:schema-evolution:v3.notes: Flink can read V3 tables with evolved schemas, but ALTER TABLE only supports changing table properties — column and partition changes are not supported via Flink DDL
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application
ALTER TABLE only supports changing table properties; column additions, renames, drops, and reordering not supported via Flink SQL

aws-managed-flink:column-default-values:.notes: Column default values are a V3 feature
Column default values are a V3 feature; Flink Iceberg integration has not explicitly documented support
caveats: No explicit documentation for Flink support of V3 column default values

aws-managed-flink:nanosecond-timestamps:.notes: Nanosecond timestamps are a V3 feature
Flink supports nanosecond timestamp precision, matching the full range of Iceberg's timestamp spec (Iceberg 1.11.0, Flink 2.1).
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application

aws-managed-flink:hidden-partitioning:v2.notes: Flink can read and write to tables with hidden partitions created by other engines, but Flink DDL does not support creating tables with transform-based (hidden) partitioning
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application
Flink DDL PARTITION BY does not support transform functions (e.g., days(), hours(), bucket()); use Spark or another engine to create hidden-partitioned tables

aws-managed-flink:hidden-partitioning:v3.notes: Flink can read and write to V3 tables with hidden partitions created by other engines, but Flink DDL does not support creating tables with transform-based (hidden) partitioning
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application
Flink DDL PARTITION BY does not support transform functions (e.g., days(), hours(), bucket()); use Spark or another engine to create hidden-partitioned tables

aws-managed-flink:partition-evolution:v2.notes: Partition evolution supported via the Dynamic Sink (Sink V2-based implementation)
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application
Requires Dynamic Sink (Sink V2-based implementation)

aws-managed-flink:partition-evolution:v3.notes: Partition evolution supported via the Dynamic Sink (Sink V2-based implementation) for V3 tables
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application
Requires Dynamic Sink (Sink V2-based implementation)

aws-managed-flink:multi-arg-transforms:v.notes: Multi-argument transforms are a V3 feature
Multi-argument transforms are a V3 feature; Flink Iceberg integration has not explicitly documented support
caveats: No explicit documentation for Flink support of V3 multi-argument transforms

aws-managed-flink:time-travel:v2.notes: Flink supports time travel via snapshot-id and as-of-timestamp read options in batch mode, plus branch and tag reads via SQL hints
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application
Uses SQL hint syntax (/*+ OPTIONS('snapshot-id'='...') */) rather than native SQL time travel syntax

aws-managed-flink:time-travel:v3.notes: Time travel supported for V3 tables via snapshot-id, as-of-timestamp, branch, and tag read options
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application
Uses SQL hint syntax (/*+ OPTIONS('snapshot-id'='...') */) rather than native SQL time travel syntax

aws-managed-flink:table-maintenance:v2.notes: Flink supports the rewrite files action (compaction) but does not support expire_snapshots, remove_orphan_files, or rewrite_manifests — use Spark for full maintenance
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application
Only rewrite files action available; expire_snapshots, remove_orphan_files, rewrite_manifests not supported

aws-managed-flink:table-maintenance:v3.notes: Flink supports the rewrite files action (compaction) for V3 tables but does not support expire_snapshots, remove_orphan_files, or rewrite_manifests
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application
Only rewrite files action available; expire_snapshots, remove_orphan_files, rewrite_manifests not supported

aws-managed-flink:branching-tagging:v2.notes: Flink can write to branches via FlinkSink.toBranch() and read from branches/tags via SQL hints, including incremental scan between tags. Cannot create or manage branches/tags via DDL
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application
Cannot create or manage branches/tags; read and write to existing branches/tags only

aws-managed-flink:branching-tagging:v3.notes: Flink can write to branches via FlinkSink.toBranch() and read from branches/tags via SQL hints for V3 tables. Cannot create or manage branches/tags via DDL
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application
Cannot create or manage branches/tags; read and write to existing branches/tags only

aws-managed-flink:read-support:v2.notes: Full read support in both batch and streaming modes, including incremental reads from snapshots and FLIP-27 source
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application

aws-managed-flink:read-support:v3.notes: Full read support for V3 tables in both batch and streaming modes via Iceberg library V3 support
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application

aws-managed-flink:write-insert:v2.notes: INSERT INTO supported for both batch and streaming modes; INSERT OVERWRITE supported in batch mode only
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application
INSERT OVERWRITE is not supported in streaming mode

aws-managed-flink:write-insert:v3.notes: INSERT INTO and INSERT OVERWRITE supported with V3 format; INSERT OVERWRITE is batch-only
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application
INSERT OVERWRITE is not supported in streaming mode

aws-managed-flink:write-merge-update-del.notes: Flink supports UPSERT mode via equality deletes (requires V2 format and primary key) but does not support SQL MERGE INTO, UPDATE, or DELETE statements
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application
No SQL MERGE INTO, UPDATE, or DELETE syntax; uses streaming upsert mode with equality deletes instead
UPSERT requires V2+ format and a PRIMARY KEY defined on the table

aws-managed-flink:write-merge-update-del.notes: Flink supports UPSERT mode via equality deletes (requires primary key) but does not support SQL MERGE INTO, UPDATE, or DELETE statements
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application
No SQL MERGE INTO, UPDATE, or DELETE syntax; uses streaming upsert mode with equality deletes instead
UPSERT requires a PRIMARY KEY defined on the table

aws-managed-flink:catalog-integration:v2.notes: Supports built-in catalog types: hive, hadoop, rest, glue, jdbc, nessie, plus custom catalog implementations via catalog-impl
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application

aws-managed-flink:catalog-integration:v3.notes: Supports built-in catalog types: hive, hadoop, rest, glue, jdbc, nessie, plus custom catalog implementations via catalog-impl
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application

caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application

caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application

caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application

aws-managed-flink:rest-catalog:v3.notes: Built-in REST Catalog support via catalog-type='rest'
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application

caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application

Requires REST Catalog endpoint configuration pointing to Unity Catalog

caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application

aws-managed-flink:jdbc-catalog:v2.notes: Built-in JDBC catalog support via catalog-type='jdbc'
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application

aws-managed-flink:jdbc-catalog:v3.notes: Built-in JDBC catalog support via catalog-type='jdbc'
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application

aws-managed-flink:table-creation:v2.notes: Flink supports creating Iceberg tables via Flink SQL DDL with CREATE TABLE, CREATE TABLE LIKE, and PARTITIONED BY (identity columns only)
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application
Hidden partitioning (transform functions) not supported in DDL; only identity partition columns

aws-managed-flink:table-creation:v3.notes: Flink supports creating V3 Iceberg tables via Flink SQL DDL (set format-version='3' in WITH clause)
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application
Hidden partitioning (transform functions) not supported in DDL; only identity partition columns

aws-managed-flink:variant-type:v2.notes: Variant Type is a V3 feature

aws-managed-flink:variant-type:v3.notes: Variant type is supported in Flink 2.1 (Iceberg 1.11.0), enabling semi-structured data ingestion and processing in Flink pipelines. Shredded Variant reads/writes are not yet documented.
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application
Shredded Variant support is not yet documented for Flink

aws-managed-flink:shredded-variant:v2.notes: Shredded Variant is a V3 feature

aws-managed-flink:shredded-variant:v3.notes: OSS Flink V3 shredded variant support is not yet documented

aws-managed-flink:geometry-type:v2.notes: Geometry Type is a V3 feature

aws-managed-flink:geometry-type:v3.notes: OSS Flink V3 geometry type support is not yet documented

aws-managed-flink:lineage:v3.notes: Flink provides row lineage readers for _row_id and _last_updated_sequence_number, with row lineage preserved through RewriteDataFiles compaction (Iceberg 1.11.0).
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application

aws-managed-flink:statistics:v2.notes: Flink Iceberg connector writes column statistics to manifest files by default via the standard Iceberg write path. Uses statistics for scan planning during reads in both batch and streaming modes.
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application

aws-managed-flink:statistics:v3.notes: Column statistics supported for V3 tables via iceberg-flink-runtime
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application

caveats: Needs further testing to confirm support

caveats: Needs further testing to confirm support

aws-managed-flink:type-promotion:v2.notes: Flink supports type widening for Iceberg tables
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application

aws-managed-flink:type-promotion:v3.notes: Flink supports type widening for Iceberg V3 tables
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application

aws-managed-flink:snowflake-horizon-cata.notes: Flink Iceberg connector supports REST catalog. Configure with catalog-type=rest and the Horizon Catalog endpoint URI.
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application

aws-managed-flink:snowflake-horizon-cata.notes: V3 support depends on iceberg-flink-runtime version (1.7.0+ recommended). REST catalog connectivity unchanged.
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application
Requires iceberg-flink-runtime 1.7.0+ for V3 table support

aws-managed-flink:deletion-vectors:v2.notes: Deletion vectors are a V3 feature; not applicable to Iceberg V2

aws-managed-flink:deletion-vectors:v3.notes: The Flink Dynamic Sink supports Iceberg V3 deletion vectors, enabling efficient row-level deletes without rewriting data files (Iceberg 1.11.0).
caveats: On Amazon Managed Flink, Iceberg is not a pre-built connector; you must bundle the iceberg-flink-runtime JAR in your application
